spec_match.rationale: The ReadableStream is constructed using a sequential pull() model with a returned Promise. Because the stream engine awaits this Promise before pulling the next chunk, the delayed chunk 3 forces chunk 4 to be enqueued late in all browsers alike. The demo fails to demonstrate actual browser-level out-of-order capabilities.

interactive.rationale: Features multiple streaming scenario buttons, a live chunk receipt log, custom metrics tracking blocks, and dynamic clear hooks.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Aligned with the parent index blurb about basic chunk-by-chunk consumption.

edge_cases.rationale: Avoids stream reading exceptions, correctly decodes raw Uint8Array buffers, and manages UI clearing hooks cleanly.

references.rationale: Cites the correct ChromeStatus reference link under see also.

design_system.rationale: Consistent with showcase requirements, employing neat ivory cards, clear margins, and a beautiful dark terminal output.

openQuestions.title: Fix sequential pull() delay logic in makeStream()
openQuestions.detail: The stream constructor on line 199 uses sequential pull() with a returned setTimeout promise. This forces chunk 4's enqueue to be delayed behind chunk 3 in all browsers, preventing the simulation from representing out-of-order execution. Rewrite the stream to enqueue chunks asynchronously from start() using parallel setTimeouts so that chunk 4 is enqueued at 400ms and chunk 3 at 1100ms, letting Chrome 150 deliver chunk 4 ahead of 3.
